English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Institute for Defense and Disarmament Studies Records 8588
26 cubic feet.
1974-2007. Collection material in English Collection includes material relating to the Nuclear Weapons Freeze Campaign (NWFC), reports, correspondence, Democratic National Committee material, Political Action Committee (PAC) material, Randy Forsberg publications and speeches and a print copy of her PhD Thesis, IDDS publications, audio/video material, print copies of the Arms Control Reporter, other administrations files. Audio recordings, computer media, videos, correspondence, printed materials, newspapers. 9865049
Biographical / Historical
The Institute for Defense and Disarmament Studies (IDDS) was a nonprofit center, founded by Dr. Randall Caroline Forsberg, that studied global military policies, arms holdings, production and trade, arms control and peace-building efforts. It also ran educational programs on current and alternative policies.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Collection includes material relating to the Nuclear Weapons Freeze Campaign (NWFC), reports, correspondence, Democratic National Committee material, Political Action Committee (PAC) material, Randy Forsberg publications and speeches and a print copy of her PhD Thesis, IDDS publications, audio/video material, print copies of the Arms Control Reporter, other administrations files.
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

Cite As:
Institute for Defense and Disarmament Studies Records, #8588. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials
See also the volume Toward a Theory of Peace : The Role of Moral Beliefs, the print version of Randall Forsberg's PhD dissertation with an introduction by Matthew Evangelista and Neta C. Crawford. Available online through the Cornell University Press. See also the Arms Control Reporter publication, JX1974.7.A73.
Conditions Governing Use
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual material that has been digitized: V-10293, V11004
Separated Materials
The Arms Control Reporter, 1982-2001, 2006, and 2007, from Boxes 22 and 25-27 have been cataloged separatley as call number JX1974.7 .A73 +. Box 25, Box 26 and Box 27 have been removed from the collection.
Related Materials
The Arms Control Reporter volumes are available online here: https://catalog.hathitrust.org/Record/000068634
